nameMeanings.name: Rodríguez
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Manuel, Luis, Carlos, Jesus, Francisco, Jorge, Pedro, Raul, Miguel, Roberto, Ramon, Rafael.  Portuguese Wenceslao, Ligia, Catarina, Paulo, Joaquim, Omero, Joao, Wenseslao, Adauto, Albeiro, Godofredo.

Spanish (Rodríguez) and Portuguese: patronymic from the personal name Rodrigo . Rodriguez (including in the sense 2 below) is the ninth most frequent surname in the US. Compare De Rodriguez and Rodriquez .

Jewish (Sephardic): adoption of the Iberian surname (see above) at the moment of conversion to Roman Catholicism. After the return to Judaism (generations later), some descendants retained the name their families used as Catholics.

variants.name: Rodiguez
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Luis, Jorge, Jose, Juan, Pedro, Raul, Adriana, Alvaro, Ana Luz, Angel, Armando, Arnulfo.
Hispanic: altered form of Spanish Rodríguez (see Rodriguez ).

variants.name: Rodriquez
meanings: Spanish (Rodríquez): variant of Rodríguez (see Rodriguez ). This surname is very rare in Spain.

variants.name: Rodriges
meanings: Hispanic: altered form of Spanish Rodríguez (see Rodriguez ).

variants.name: Rodreguez
meanings: Hispanic: altered form of Spanish Rodríguez (see Rodriguez ).

variants.name: Rodrigo
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Manuel, Elena, Rafael, Alfonso, Alicia, Angel, Araceli, Benardo, Carlos, Carlota, Cristina, Emilio.  Indian Devi, Dileep, Dilip, Gamini, Mahendra, Manoj, Sunil, Tilak.

Spanish and Portuguese: from the personal name Rodrigo, from ancient Germanic Hrōdrīc, a compound of hrōd ‘fame, renown’ + rīc ‘power, kingdom’. Rodrigo is one of the most important Spanish personal names of ancient Germanic origin. It was borne by the last of the Visigoth kings and by a Christian saint, Saint Roderick, one of the 9th-century martyrs of Córdoba. This surname is also found in and around some of the former Portuguese territories of western India.

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.
Indian: variant of   Dey  .

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Guzmán
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Jose, Juan, Luis, Carlos, Manuel, Jesus, Francisco, Miguel, Pedro, Rafael, Ramon, Raul.
Spanish (Guzmán): of uncertain and disputed etymology, probably from an ancient Germanic personal name. Compare De Guzman .
Jewish (eastern Ashkenazic): variant of   Gusman  .

variants.name: De Guzman
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Jose, Ernesto, Pedro, Manuel, Reynaldo, Ricardo, Jaime, Alberto, Alfredo, Edgardo, Francisco, Mario.
Spanish (De Guzmán): patronymic from Guzmán (see Guzman ), with the preposition de ‘of’.

variants.name: Gusman
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Jose, Carlos, Juan, Blanca, Jorge, Manuel, Salvador, Santiago, Adolfo, Alejandro, Alfonso, Alvino.
Hispanic (mainly Mexico; Gusmán): altered form of Spanish Guzmán (see Guzman ).
Jewish (eastern Ashkenazic): occupational name for a metalworker, from Yiddish gus ‘casting’ + man ‘man’.

nameMeanings.name: Pedro
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: From Greek. See Peter .

variants.name: Peter
meanings: form of the name of the best-known of all Christ's apostles, traditionally regarded as the founder of the Christian Church. The name derives, via Latin, from Greek petros ‘stone, rock’. This is used as a translation of the Aramaic byname Cephas, given to the apostle Simon son of Jona, to distinguish him from another of the same name ( Simon Zelotes ). ‘When Jesus beheld him, he said, Thou art Simon the son of Jona: thou shalt be called Cephas, which is by interpretation, A stone’ (John 1:42). According to Matthew 16:17–18, Christ says more explicitly, ‘Blessed art thou, Simon Bar-jona … thou art Peter, and upon this rock I will build my church’. The name has been in continuous use since the Middle Ages.

en.lifeSummary: When Don Pedro Rodríguez de Guzmán was born about 1135, his father, Rodrigo Núñez de Guzmán, was 16 and his mother, Mayor Diaz, was 11. He had at least 1 son and 1 daughter with Mayor Diaz. He died on 19 July 1195, in Ciudad Real, Castilla-La Mancha, Spain, at the age of 61.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Don Pedro Rodríguez de Guzmán est né environ 1135, son père, Rodrigo Núñez de Guzmán, avait 16 ans et sa mère, Mayor Diaz, avait 11 ans. Il a eu au moins 1 fils et 1 fille avec Mayor Diaz. Il est décédé le 19 juillet 1195, à Ciudad Real, Castilla-La Mancha, Espagne, à l’âge de 61 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Als Don Pedro Rodríguez de Guzmán um 1135 geboren wurde, war sein Vater Rodrigo Núñez de Guzmán 16 und seine Mutter Mayor Diaz 11. Er hatte mit Mayor Diaz mindestens 1 Sohn und 1 Tochter. Er starb am 19. Juli 1195 im Alter von 61 Jahren in Ciudad Real, Castilla-La Mancha, Spanien.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Don Pedro Rodríguez de Guzmán è nato il circa 1135, il suo padre, Rodrigo Núñez de Guzmán, aveva 16 anni e il suo madre, Mayor Diaz, aveva 11 anni. Lui avuto almeno 1 figlio e 1 figlia con Mayor Diaz. Morì il 19 luglio 1195, nel Ciudad Real, Castilla-La Mancha, Spagna, all'età di 61 anni.

ko.lifeSummary: Don Pedro Rodríguez de Guzmán(이)가 대략 1135년에 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Rodrigo Núñez de Guzmán(은)는 16세였고, 그의 어머니 Mayor Diaz(은)는 11세였다. 그는 Mayor Diaz(와)과 적어도 한 명의 아들과 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1195년 7월 19일 스페인 카스틸라-라 먼차 시우다드 리에서 61세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Don Pedro Rodríguez de Guzmán nasceu aproximadamente 1135, seu pai, Rodrigo Núñez de Guzmán, tinha 16 anos e sua mãe, Mayor Diaz, tinha 11 anos. Ele teve pelo menos 1 filho e 1 filha com Mayor Diaz. Ele faleceu em 19 de julho de 1195, em Ciudad Real, Castilla-La Mancha, Espanha, com 61 anos.

ru.Castrojeriz, Burgos, Castilla y León, Es: кастроджириз, бургос, кастилла и леoн, Испания
ru.lifeSummary: Когда Don Pedro Rodríguez de Guzmán родился около 1135 г., его отцу по имени Rodrigo Núñez de Guzmán было 16 лет, а матери, которую звали Mayor Diaz, 11. У него был по крайней мере 1 сын и 1 дочь от Mayor Diaz. Он умер 19 июля 1195 г. в местности сюдад риль, кастилла-ла манча, Испания в возрасте 61 год.
